description_long: This object is a weathered, partially damaged airplane with a twin-engine configuration mounted under straight, low-set wings. The fuselage is relatively short and robust, with a forward-positioned, enclosed cockpit featuring multiple windows. The wings and tail surfaces show signs of deterioration, with jagged edges and missing sections, suggesting the aircraft has been abandoned or crashed. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers in a conventional arrangement. The exterior surface is predominantly a faded, light color with patches of corrosion and debris, giving it a distressed appearance. The airplane's overall structure is metallic, with visible rivets and seams along the body and wings.
description: A weathered, twin-engine airplane with straight low wings, a forward enclosed cockpit, and a damaged, corroded exterior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, twin engines under the wings, and significant wing damage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the short fuselage, cockpit placement, and the jagged, deteriorated trailing edge of the wing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and the extent of wing and tail damage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the fuselage windows, cockpit, and more visible damage to the wing and tail surfaces.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted under each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear and a nose gear, partially visible and appears fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
